summary: color-style-utils ships postinstall scripts that collect host data and execute system commands
details: The package ships two postinstall scripts (postinstall.js and postinstall2.js) that import child_process and perform outbound HTTP/HTTPS requests at install time. postinstall.js makes http.request calls and POSTs data including a hostname field collected at multiple call sites. postinstall2.js similarly uses http.request/https.request with a POST and additionally invokes execSync('netsh...'), a Windows networking command unrelated to any plausible 'color style' utility. The combined shape — install-time child_process + outbound HTTP + system-info collection (hostname) + Windows network configuration commands — in a package whose advertised purpose is color/style helpers is consistent with installer-side host reconnaissance and exfiltration on npm install. The package name and stated purpose provide no legitimate reason for postinstall network activity, system command execution, or hostname harvesting.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
